     1→permalink: /posts/programming/ai-era-refactoring-from-rfc-to-five-plans
     2→---
     3→
     4→
     5→## 动机
     6→
     7→在这个人人都能使用 AI 创造新产品的年代，认真分享一次 AI 帮助我们改进代码、完成一整个完整重构工作流的经历，想必还是非常难得的。
     8→
     9→在这里，我想分享一下我如何在较短的时间内完成 LobeHub 这个项目。我将 Next.js 承载的大部分页面从 Next App Router 迁移到使用 Vite 来承载，以及迁移之后它是如何工作的。
    10→
    11→整个方案设计从初步构思到最终验收，是如何一步步推进的？
    12→
    13→这次重构的背景是由于 LobeHub 这个体量的项目，在使用 Next.js 承载所有前端页面时，开发环境启动并渲染首屏后，`next-server` 的内存占用差不多就要达到 12GB。
    14→
    15→这对很多开发者来说都不是一件很友好的事情，尤其是还在使用 16GB 内存的开发者。我也算是一个受害者，因为我之前的电脑内存也只有 32GB，相当于我不能同时开启多个开发环境。光是这一个 Dev Server 跑起来，我的电脑内存几乎就已经满了，何况我们在开发过程中还会使用 Chrome 打开大量的 Tab。
    16→
    17→基于以上事实，迁移到 Vite 会是一个更好的选择。
    18→
    19→首先，这个项目的背景是在半年时间之前，已经开始尝试迁移到 SPA。
    20→
    21→在它的路由中，大部分路由都是由 React Router 进行承载，但它运行在 Next.js 的路由体系内，这就形成了一种“一国两制”的局面：
    22→
    23→1. 既存在 Next.js 的完整路由系统
    24→2. 又存在着 React Router SPA 的路由系统
    25→
    26→在不少组件中，还存在混用 react-router-dom 的 Link 组件的情况，当然也有可能在使用 Next 提供的 Link 组件。
    27→
    28→## 前置知识
    29→
    30→### `[variants]`与静态 HTML 入口
    31→
    32→在正式开始之前，需要先理解 `src/app/[variants]` 这个设计。
    33→
    34→LobeHub 需要根据用户的**语言（locale）**和**设备类型（isMobile）**，在首屏返回不同的 HTML、SEO 元数据和 viewport 配置。为了避免运行时计算，项目把这两个维度编码进了 URL 路径：格式为 `{locale}__{isMobile}`，比如 `zh-CN__0`、`en-US__1`。构建时通过 `generateStaticParams` 枚举所有组合，Web 构建会产出 4 份静态 HTML；用户访问时，Middleware 在边缘层完成解析和 rewrite，CDN 直接命中对应产物返回。

     6→
     7→import { clsxm } from '~/lib/helper'
     8→import { WrappedElementProvider } from '~/providers/shared/WrappedElementProvider'
     9→
    10→import { Banner } from '../../banner/Banner'
    11→import { Gallery } from '../../gallery/Gallery'
    12→import { Markdown } from '../Markdown'
    13→import { GridMarkdownImage, GridMarkdownImages } from '../renderers/image'
    14→import { pickImagesFromMarkdown } from '../utils/image'
    15→
    16→const shouldCatchContainerName = [
    17→  'gallery',
    18→  'banner',
    19→  'carousel',
    20→
    21→  'warn',
    22→  'error',
    23→  'danger',
    24→  'info',
    25→  'success',
    26→  'warning',
    27→  'note',
    28→
    29→  'grid',
    30→  'masonry',
    31→].join('|')
    32→
    33→export const ContainerRule: MarkdownToJSX.Rule<{
    34→  node: {
    35→    type: string
    36→    params: string
    37→    content: string
    38→  }
    39→}> = {
    40→  match: (source: string) => {
    41→    const result =
    42→      /^\s*::: *(?<type>.*?) *(?:{(?<params>.*?)} *)?\n(?<content>[\S\s]+?)\s*::: *(?:\n *)+/.exec(
    43→        source,
    44→      )
    45→
    46→    if (!result) return null
    47→
    48→    const { type } = result.groups!
    49→    if (!type || !type.match(shouldCatchContainerName)) return null
    50→    return result
    51→  },
    52→  order: Priority.MED,
    53→  parse(capture) {
    54→    const { groups } = capture
    55→    return {
    56→      node: { ...groups } as any,
    57→    }
    58→  },
    59→
    60→  render(node, _, state) {
    61→    const { type, params, content } = node.node
    62→
    63→    switch (type) {
    64→      case 'carousel':
    65→      case 'gallery': {
    66→        return (
    67→          <Gallery images={pickImagesFromMarkdown(content)} key={state?.key} />
    68→        )
    69→      }
    70→      case 'warn':
    71→      case 'error':
    72→      case 'danger':
    73→      case 'info':
    74→      case 'note':
    75→      case 'success':
    76→      case 'warning': {
    77→        const transformMap = {
    78→          warning: 'warn',
    79→          danger: 'error',
    80→          note: 'info',
    81→        }
    82→        return (
    83→          <Banner
    84→            className="my-4"
    85→            key={state?.key}
    86→            type={(transformMap as any)[type] || type}
    87→          >
    88→            <WrappedElementProvider className="w-full">
    89→              <Markdown
    90→                allowsScript
    91→                className="w-full [&>p:first-child]:mt-0"
    92→                value={content}
    93→              />
    94→            </WrappedElementProvider>
    95→          </Banner>
    96→        )
    97→      }
    98→      case 'banner': {
    99→        if (!params) {
   100→          break
   101→        }
   102→
   103→        return (
   104→          <Banner
   105→            className="my-4"
   106→            key={state?.key}
   107→            type={params as 'warn' | 'error' | 'info' | 'success' | 'warning'}
   108→          >
   109→            <WrappedElementProvider className="w-full">
   110→              <Markdown
   111→                allowsScript
   112→                className="w-full [&>p:first-child]:mt-0"
   113→                value={content}
   114→              />
   115→            </WrappedElementProvider>
   116→          </Banner>
   117→        )
   118→      }
   119→
   120→      case 'grid': {
   121→        // cols=2,gap=4,rows=2,type=images
   122→
   123→        const { cols, gap = 8, rows, type = 'normal' } = parseParams(params)
   124→
   125→        const Grid: Component = ({ children, className }) => (
   126→          <div
   127→            className={clsxm('relative grid w-full', className)}
   128→            style={{
   129→              gridTemplateColumns: cols
   130→                ? `repeat(${cols}, minmax(0, 1fr))`
   131→                : undefined,
   132→              gap: `${gap}px`,
   133→              gridTemplateRows: rows
   134→                ? `repeat(${rows}, minmax(0, 1fr))`
   135→                : undefined,
   136→            }}
   137→          >
   138→            {children}
   139→          </div>
   140→        )
   141→        switch (type) {
   142→          case 'normal': {
   143→            return (
   144→              <Grid key={state?.key}>
   145→                <Markdown
   146→                  allowsScript
   147→                  removeWrapper
   148→                  className="w-full [&>p:first-child]:mt-0"
   149→                  value={content}
   150→                  overrides={{
   151→                    img: GridMarkdownImage,
   152→                  }}
   153→                />
   154→              </Grid>
   155→            )
   156→          }
   157→          case 'images': {
   158→            const imagesSrc = pickImagesFromMarkdown(content).map((r) => r.url)
   159→
   160→            return (
   161→              <GridMarkdownImages
   162→                Wrapper={Grid}
   163→                height={rows && cols ? +rows / +cols : 1}
   164→                imagesSrc={imagesSrc}
   165→                key={state?.key}
   166→              />
   167→            )
   168→          }
   169→          default: {
   170→            return null
   171→          }
   172→        }
   173→      }
   174→      case 'masonry': {
   175→        const { gap = 8 } = parseParams(params)
   176→        const imagesSrc = pickImagesFromMarkdown(content).map((r) => r.url)
   177→
   178→        return (
   179→          <ResponsiveMasonry
   180→            columnsCountBreakPoints={{
   181→              350: 1,
   182→              750: 2,
   183→            }}
   184→          >
   185→            <Masonry className="[&_figure]:my-0" gutter={`${gap}px`}>
   186→              {imagesSrc.map((src) => (
   187→                <GridMarkdownImage key={src} src={src} />
   188→              ))}
   189→            </Masonry>
   190→          </ResponsiveMasonry>
   191→        )
   192→      }
   193→    }
   194→
   195→    return (
   196→      <div key={state?.key}>
   197→        <p>{content}</p>
   198→      </div>
   199→    )
   200→  },
   201→}
   202→
   203→/**
   204→ * gallery container
   205→ *
   206→ * ::: gallery
   207→ * ![name](url)
   208→ * ![name](url)
   209→ * ![name](url)
   210→ * :::
   211→ */
   212→
   213→type ParsedResult = Record<string, string>
   214→
   215→function parseParams(input: string): ParsedResult {
   216→  const regex = /(\w+)=(\w+)/g
   217→  let match: RegExpExecArray | null
   218→  const result: ParsedResult = {}
   219→
   220→  while ((match = regex.exec(input)) !== null) {
   221→    const key = match[1]
   222→    const value = match[2]
   223→    result[key] = value
   224→  }
   225→
   226→  return result
   227→}
   228→

     5→  children: string,

     8→  const isCurrentlyInline = state.inline || false
     9→  const isCurrentlySimple = state.simple || false
    10→  state.inline = true
    11→  state.simple = true
    12→  const result = parse(children, state)

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-03-11 12:38:32Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="write" data-tool-name="Write"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Write**</summary>

```mjs
import { readFileSync } from 'fs'
import { fileURLToPath } from 'url'
import { dirname, join } from 'path'

const __dirname = dirname(fileURLToPath(import.meta.url))
const testMd = readFileSync(join(__dirname, 'test.md'), 'utf-8')

// Monkey-patch RegExp.prototype.exec to track slow regexes
const originalExec = RegExp.prototype.exec
const regexStats = new Map()
const TIMEOUT_MS = 500

let callCount = 0

RegExp.prototype.exec = function(str) {
  callCount++
  const key = this.toString()
  const inputPreview = typeof str === 'string' ? str.slice(0, 80) : '<non-string>'
  const start = performance.now()

  // Set a timeout to detect hangs
  const result = originalExec.call(this, str)

  const elapsed = performance.now() - start

  if (elapsed > 5) {
    const entry = regexStats.get(key) || { totalTime: 0, calls: 0, maxTime: 0, maxInput: '', regex: key }
    entry.totalTime += elapsed
    entry.calls++
    if (elapsed > entry.maxTime) {
      entry.maxTime = elapsed
      entry.maxInput = inputPreview
      entry.inputLen = typeof str === 'string' ? str.length : 0
    }
    regexStats.set(key, entry)
  }

  if (elapsed > TIMEOUT_MS) {
    console.error(`\n🚨 SLOW REGEX DETECTED (${elapsed.toFixed(1)}ms):`)
    console.error(`  Pattern: ${key}`)
    console.error(`  Input length: ${typeof str === 'string' ? str.length : 'N/A'}`)
    console.error(`  Input preview: ${inputPreview}...`)
  }

  return result
}

console.log('Starting markdown compilation with regex profiling...')
console.log(`Input length: ${testMd.length} chars\n`)

const startTime = performance.now()

// Dynamically import markdown-to-jsx after monkey-patching
const { compiler, RuleType } = await import('markdown-to-jsx')

try {
  // Set a generous timeout - if it takes more than 30s, something is wrong
  const timeoutId = setTimeout(() => {
    console.error('\n⏰ TIMEOUT: Compilation took > 30s, dumping stats and exiting')
    dumpStats()
    process.exit(1)
  }, 30000)

  compiler(testMd, {
    overrides: {},
    extendsRules: {},
  })

  clearTimeout(timeoutId)

  const totalTime = performance.now() - startTime
  console.log(`\nCompilation completed in ${totalTime.toFixed(1)}ms`)
  console.log(`Total regex exec calls: ${callCount}`)
} catch (e) {
  console.error('Compilation error:', e.message)
}

dumpStats()

function dumpStats() {
  console.log('\n=== Slow Regex Report (>5ms per call) ===\n')

  const sorted = [...regexStats.values()].sort((a, b) => b.totalTime - a.totalTime)

  for (const entry of sorted.slice(0, 20)) {
    console.log(`Total: ${entry.totalTime.toFixed(1)}ms | Max: ${entry.maxTime.toFixed(1)}ms | Calls: ${entry.calls}`)
    console.log(`  Pattern: ${entry.regex}`)
    console.log(`  Worst input len: ${entry.inputLen}`)
    console.log(`  Worst input: ${entry.maxInput}...`)
    console.log()
  }
}

RegExp.prototype.exec = originalExec

```

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-03-11 12:41:20Z)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
Found it! The `INLINE_SKIP_R` in `markdown-to-jsx` is:

```
((?:\\[.*?\\][([].*?[)\\]]|<.*?>(?:.*?<.*?>)?|`.*?`|\\\\\\1|[\\s\\S])+?)
```

This is a complex alternation inside a `()+?` group. The `[\\s\\S]` at the end is a catch-all that matches any character, and when combined with the other alternatives and the lazy quantifier, it can cause catastrophic backtracking.

The issue is that `[\s\S]` overlaps with all the other alternatives. 

When the regex engine encounters a string without the expected closing delimiter, it has to explore exponentially many ways to partition the input across the different alternatives. I can see the project's custom parsers already use a simpler non-backtracking pattern to avoid this, but the `markdown-to-jsx` library's own implementation hasn't been updated with the same fix.

Now I need to write a test to pinpoint exactly which regex is causing the hang, so I'm examining the test.md file more closely to see what content triggers the issue.
</details></think>

```
((?:\\[.*?\\][([].*?[)\\]]|<.*?>(?:.*?<.*?>)?|`.*?`|\\\\\\1|[\\s\\S])+?)
```

此正则有多个重叠 alternative（`[\s\S]` 与其余均重叠），外层 `()+?` lazy quantifier 导致 catastrophic backtracking。

     7→在这个人人都能使用 AI 创造新产品的年代，认真分享一次 AI 帮助我们改进代码、完成一整个完整重构工作流的经历，想必还是非常难得的。
     8→
     9→在这里，我想分享一下我如何在较短的时间内完成 LobeHub 这个项目。我将 Next.js 承载的大部分页面从 Next App Router 迁移到使用 Vite 来承载，以及迁移之后它是如何工作的。
    10→
    11→整个方案设计从初步构思到最终验收，是如何一步步推进的？
    12→
    13→这次重构的背景是由于 LobeHub 这个体量的项目，在使用 Next.js 承载所有前端页面时，开发环境启动并渲染首屏后，`next-server` 的内存占用差不多就要达到 12GB。
    14→
    15→这对很多开发者来说都不是一件很友好的事情，尤其是还在使用 16GB 内存的开发者。我也算是一个受害者，因为我之前的电脑内存也只有 32GB，相当于我不能同时开启多个开发环境。光是这一个 Dev Server 跑起来，我的电脑内存几乎就已经满了，何况我们在开发过程中还会使用 Chrome 打开大量的 Tab。
    16→
    17→基于以上事实，迁移到 Vite 会是一个更好的选择。
    18→
    19→首先，这个项目的背景是在半年时间之前，已经开始尝试迁移到 SPA。
    20→
    21→在它的路由中，大部分路由都是由 React Router 进行承载，但它运行在 Next.js 的路由体系内，这就形成了一种“一国两制”的局面：
    22→
    23→1. 既存在 Next.js 的完整路由系统
    24→2. 又存在着 React Router SPA 的路由系统
    25→
    26→在不少组件中，还存在混用 react-router-dom 的 Link 组件的情况，当然也有可能在使用 Next 提供的 Link 组件。
    27→
    28→## 前置知识
    29→
    30→### `[variants]`与静态 HTML 入口
    31→
    32→在正式开始之前，需要先理解 `src/app/[variants]` 这个设计。
    33→
    34→LobeHub 需要根据用户的**语言（locale）**和**设备类型（isMobile）**，在首屏返回不同的 HTML、SEO 元数据和 viewport 配置。为了避免运行时计算，项目把这两个维度编码进了 URL 路径：格式为 `{locale}__{isMobile}`，比如 `zh-CN__0`、`en-US__1`。构建时通过 `generateStaticParams` 枚举所有组合，Web 构建会产出 4 份静态 HTML；用户访问时，Middleware 在边缘层完成解析和 rewrite，CDN 直接命中对应产物返回。
    35→
    36→<excalidraw>
<!-- [omitted 12001 chars — likely node_modules or generated bundle] -->

    39→
    40→
    41→理解这套机制，也就理解了迁移到 Vite 的难点一：Vite 默认只产出一个 `index.html`，不会替你生成这份笛卡尔积。如何在 Vite 侧复现等价的多 HTML 产物，是整个方案要解决的第一个问题。
    42→
    43→### Next.js 作为资源反向代理
    44→
    45→LobeHub 是一个全栈应用，所有 API 都跑在 Next.js 上。迁移完成后，Next.js Server 不会消失——它只是从「承载页面」变成「纯后端服务」。
    46→
    47→但 Vite 构建出的静态资源，仍然需要有人来托管和分发。最直觉的做法是在网关层用 Nginx 做路径映射，但这条路走不通，原因有两个：一是项目部署在 Vercel 上，没有地方插 Nginx；二是上一节提到的 Route Variants——`/settings` 到 `/zh-CN__0/settings` 的 rewrite 逻辑必须在 Middleware 层执行，而 Middleware 就跑在 Next.js 里。
    48→
    49→所以最终的选择是：**让 Next.js Server 同时承担静态资源的反向代理**。

Actually, wait. Let me think more carefully. The `INLINE_SKIP_R` is:```


((?:\\[.*?\\][([].*?[)\\]]|<.*?>(?:.*?<.*?>)?|`.*?`|\\\\\\1|[\\s\\S])+?)
```

This pattern is used in several text formatting regexes for bold, italic, marked, and strikethrough text. The issue is that this pattern tries to match content between delimiters, but with the large `<excalidraw>` block containing nested angle brackets and complex JSON, the regex engine could get caught in catastrophic backtracking when trying to match the `<.*?>(?:.*?<.*?>)?` part.

Looking at line 34 in the test file, there's a line starting with `*` that contains bold text markers with `**`, but the real problem is likely that the regex is trying to match across the massive `<excalidraw>` block that starts earlier in the file. When the engine looks for a closing delimiter and encounters all those nested structures, the overlapping alternatives in `INLINE_SKIP_R` cause exponential backtracking attempts. 我在分析这个正则表达式的问题——它用来匹配 HTML 块元素，但在处理 `<excalidraw>` 标签内的大型 JSON 内容时可能会遇到困难。正则表达式中的嵌套量词和非贪心匹配可能导致性能问题或匹配失败，特别是当 JSON 数据很庞大时。

让我检查一下实际的标签结构，看看闭合标签是否在预期的位置。

这个正则表达式在处理嵌套的 `excalidraw` 标签时，通过逐字符匹配内容，可能会导致灾难性的回溯问题，特别是当闭合标签不在预期位置时。

<tool-use data-tool-type="write" data-tool-name="Write"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Write**</summary>

```mjs
import { readFileSync } from 'fs'
import { fileURLToPath } from 'url'
import { dirname, join } from 'path'
import { Worker, isMainThread, parentPort, workerData } from 'worker_threads'

const __dirname = dirname(fileURLToPath(import.meta.url))

if (!isMainThread) {
  // Worker: test a single regex against a single input
  const { pattern, flags, input } = workerData
  const regex = new RegExp(pattern, flags)
  const start = performance.now()
  const result = regex.exec(input)
  const elapsed = performance.now() - start
  parentPort.postMessage({ elapsed, matched: !!result })
  process.exit(0)
}

// Main thread
const testMd = readFileSync(join(__dirname, 'test.md'), 'utf-8')

// All regexes from markdown-to-jsx that could be problematic
const INLINE_SKIP_R = '((?:\\\\\\[.*?\\\\\\][([].*?[)\\\\\\]]|<.*?>(?:.*?<.*?>)?|`.*?`|\\\\\\\\\\1|[\\s\\S])+?)'

const regexes = {
  'ATTR_EXTENDER_R': { pattern: '([-A-Z0-9_:]+)(?:\\s*=\\s*(?:(?:"((?:\\\\.|[^"])*)")|(?:\'((?:\\\\.|[^\'])*)\')|(?:\\{((?:\\\\.|{[^}]*?}|[^}])*)\\})))?', flags: 'gi' },
  'BLOCKQUOTE_R': { pattern: '^(\\s*>[\\s\\S]*?)(?=\\n\\n|$)', flags: '' },
  'CODE_BLOCK_FENCED_R': { pattern: '^(?: {1,3})?(`{3,}|~{3,}) *(\\S+)? *([^\\n]*?)?\\n([\\s\\S]*?)(?:\\1\\n?|$)', flags: '' },
  'CODE_BLOCK_R': { pattern: '^(?: {4}[^\\n]+\\n*)+(?:\\n *)+\\n?', flags: '' },
  'CODE_INLINE_R': { pattern: '^(`+)((?:\\\\`|(?!\\1)`|[^`])+)\\1', flags: '' },
  'FOOTNOTE_R': { pattern: '^\\[\\^([^\\]]+)](:(.*)((\n+ {4,}.*)|(\n(?!\\[\\^).+))*)', flags: '' },
  'FRONT_MATTER_R': { pattern: '^---[ \\t]*\\n(.|\\n)*\\n---[ \\t]*\\n', flags: '' },
  'HEADING_R': { pattern: '^ *(#{1,6}) *([^\\n]+?)(?: +#*)?(?:\\n *)*(?:\\n|$)', flags: '' },
  'HEADING_SETEXT_R': { pattern: '^([^\\n]+)\\n *(=|-){3,} *(?:\\n *)+\\n', flags: '' },
  'HTML_BLOCK_ELEMENT_R': { pattern: '^ *(?!<[a-z][^ >/]* ?\\/>)<([a-z][^ >/]*) ?((?:[^>]*[^/])?)>\\n?(\\s*(?:<\\1[^>]*?>[\\s\\S]*?<\\/\\1>|(?!<\\1\\b)[\\s\\S])*?)<\\/\\1>(?!<\\/\\1>)\\n*', flags: 'i' },
  'HTML_SELF_CLOSING_R': { pattern: '^ *<([a-z][a-z0-9:]*)(?:\\s+((?:<.*?>|[^>])*))?\\/>(?!<\\/\\1>)(\\s*\\n)?', flags: 'i' },
  'HTML_COMMENT_R': { pattern: '^<!--[\\s\\S]*?(?:-->)', flags: '' },
  'IMAGE_R': { pattern: '^!\\[(.*?)\\]\\( *((?:\\([^)]*\\)|[^() ])*)\\s*"?([^)"]*)?\"?\\)', flags: '' },
  'NP_TABLE_R': { pattern: '^(\\|.*)\\n(?: *(\\|? *[-:]+ *\\|[-| :]*)\\n((?:.*\\|.*\\n)*))?\\n?', flags: '' },
  'PARAGRAPH_R': { pattern: '^[^\\n]+(?:  \\n|\\n{2,})', flags: '' },
  'TEXT_BOLD_R': { pattern: '^([*_])\\1' + INLINE_SKIP_R + '\\1\\1(?!\\1)', flags: '' },
  'TEXT_EMPHASIZED_R': { pattern: '^([*_])' + INLINE_SKIP_R + '\\1(?!\\1)', flags: '' },
  'TEXT_MARKED_R': { pattern: '^(==)' + INLINE_SKIP_R + '\\1', flags: '' },
  'TEXT_STRIKETHROUGHED_R': { pattern: '^(~~)' + INLINE_SKIP_R + '\\1', flags: '' },
  'TEXT_PLAIN_R': { pattern: '^(?: |[\\s\\S](?:(?!  |[0-9]\\.)[^=*_~\\-\\n<`\\\\\\[! ])*)', flags: '' },
  'LINK_AUTOLINK_BARE_URL_R': { pattern: '^(https?:\\/\\/[^\\s<]+[^<.,:;"\')\\]\\s])', flags: '' },
  'CONTAINER_R': { pattern: '^\\s*::: *(?<type>.*?) *(?:{(?<params>.*?)} *)?\\n(?<content>[\\S\\s]+?)\\s*::: *(?:\\n *)+', flags: '' },
}

// Split test.md into paragraphs/chunks for testing
const chunks = testMd.split(/\n\n+/)

console.log(`Testing ${Object.keys(regexes).length} regexes against ${chunks.length} chunks\n`)

const TIMEOUT_MS = 3000

async function testRegex(name, { pattern, flags }, input, chunkIdx) {
  return new Promise((resolve) => {
    const worker = new Worker(fileURLToPath(import.meta.url), {
      workerData: { pattern, flags, input }
    })

    const timer = setTimeout(() => {
      worker.terminate()
      resolve({ name, chunkIdx, elapsed: TIMEOUT_MS, timedOut: true, inputLen: input.length, inputPreview: input.slice(0, 120) })
    }, TIMEOUT_MS)

    worker.on('message', (msg) => {
      clearTimeout(timer)
      resolve({ name, chunkIdx, ...msg, inputLen: input.length, inputPreview: input.slice(0, 120) })
    })

    worker.on('error', (err) => {
      clearTimeout(timer)
      resolve({ name, chunkIdx, error: err.message, inputLen: input.length })
    })
  })
}

const slowResults = []

for (const [name, regexDef] of Object.entries(regexes)) {
  for (let i = 0; i < chunks.length; i++) {
    const chunk = chunks[i]
    if (!chunk.trim()) continue

    // For inline regexes, test against each line too
    const inputs = [chunk]
    if (name.startsWith('TEXT_') || name === 'CODE_INLINE_R') {
      for (const line of chunk.split('\n')) {
        if (line.trim()) inputs.push(line)
      }
    }

    for (const input of inputs) {
      const result = await testRegex(name, regexDef, input, i)
      if (result.timedOut) {
        console.error(`🚨 TIMEOUT: ${name} on chunk ${i} (${result.inputLen} chars)`)
        console.error(`   Preview: ${result.inputPreview}...`)
        slowResults.push(result)
        // Skip remaining inputs for this regex+chunk combo
        break
      } else if (result.elapsed > 50) {
        console.warn(`⚠️  SLOW: ${name} took ${result.elapsed.toFixed(1)}ms on chunk ${i} (${result.inputLen} chars)`)
        slowResults.push(result)
      }
    }
  }
  process.stdout.write(`.`)
}

console.log('\n\n=== Results ===\n')
if (slowResults.length === 0) {
  console.log('No slow regexes found!')
} else {
  for (const r of slowResults.sort((a, b) => b.elapsed - a.elapsed)) {
    console.log(`${r.timedOut ? '🚨 TIMEOUT' : '⚠️  SLOW'}: ${r.name} | ${r.elapsed.toFixed(0)}ms | chunk ${r.chunkIdx} | ${r.inputLen} chars`)
    if (r.inputPreview) console.log(`   ${r.inputPreview}...`)
    console.log()
  }
}

import { readFileSync } from 'fs'
import { fileURLToPath } from 'url'
import { dirname, join } from 'path'
import { Worker, isMainThread, parentPort, workerData } from 'worker_threads'

const __dirname = dirname(fileURLToPath(import.meta.url))

if (!isMainThread) {
  // Worker: compile markdown
  const { input } = workerData
  const { compiler } = await import('markdown-to-jsx')
  const start = performance.now()
  try {
    compiler(input)
  } catch {}
  parentPort.postMessage({ elapsed: performance.now() - start })
  process.exit(0)
}

const testMd = readFileSync(join(__dirname, 'test.md'), 'utf-8')
const TIMEOUT_MS = 5000

async function tryCompile(input, label) {
  return new Promise((resolve) => {
    const worker = new Worker(fileURLToPath(import.meta.url), {
      workerData: { input }
    })

    const timer = setTimeout(() => {
      worker.terminate()
      resolve({ timedOut: true, label })
    }, TIMEOUT_MS)

    worker.on('message', (msg) => {
      clearTimeout(timer)
      resolve({ ...msg, timedOut: false, label })
    })

    worker.on('error', (err) => {
      clearTimeout(timer)
      resolve({ error: err.message, timedOut: false, label })
    })
  })
}

// First, confirm full file hangs
console.log('Testing full file...')
let result = await tryCompile(testMd, 'full')
if (result.timedOut) {
  console.log(`🚨 Full file HANGS (>${TIMEOUT_MS}ms)`)
} else {
  console.log(`Full file compiled in ${result.elapsed.toFixed(0)}ms - no hang detected!`)
  process.exit(0)
}

// Binary search by paragraphs
const paragraphs = testMd.split(/\n\n+/)
console.log(`\nTotal paragraphs: ${paragraphs.length}`)
console.log('Binary search for hanging section...\n')

let lo = 0, hi = paragraphs.length

// First find the minimum prefix of paragraphs that hangs
while (lo < hi) {
  const mid = Math.floor((lo + hi) / 2)
  const slice = paragraphs.slice(0, mid + 1).join('\n\n')
  result = await tryCompile(slice, `paragraphs 0-${mid}`)

  if (result.timedOut) {
    console.log(`🚨 paragraphs 0-${mid} HANGS (${slice.length} chars)`)
    hi = mid
  } else {
    console.log(`✅ paragraphs 0-${mid} OK (${result.elapsed.toFixed(0)}ms, ${slice.length} chars)`)
    lo = mid + 1
  }
}

console.log(`\n=== Hanging paragraph index: ${lo} ===`)
console.log(`Preview: ${paragraphs[lo]?.slice(0, 200)}...`)
console.log(`Full paragraph length: ${paragraphs[lo]?.length}`)

// Now test: everything before this paragraph should be fine,
// and adding this paragraph causes the hang
const beforeHang = paragraphs.slice(0, lo).join('\n\n')
const hangParagraph = paragraphs[lo]

console.log('\n--- Testing paragraph alone ---')
result = await tryCompile(hangParagraph, 'paragraph alone')
if (result.timedOut) {
  console.log(`🚨 Paragraph alone HANGS`)
} else {
  console.log(`✅ Paragraph alone OK (${result.elapsed.toFixed(0)}ms)`)
  console.log('=> The hang is caused by interaction with preceding content')

  // Try adding just the previous paragraph
  for (let i = Math.max(0, lo - 5); i < lo; i++) {
    const combo = paragraphs.slice(i, lo + 1).join('\n\n')
    result = await tryCompile(combo, `paragraphs ${i}-${lo}`)
    if (result.timedOut) {
      console.log(`🚨 paragraphs ${i}-${lo} HANGS (${combo.length} chars)`)
      console.log(`\nParagraph ${i}: ${paragraphs[i]?.slice(0, 200)}...`)
    } else {
      console.log(`✅ paragraphs ${i}-${lo} OK (${result.elapsed.toFixed(0)}ms)`)
    }
  }
}

// Also: try to narrow down within the hanging paragraph by lines
if (hangParagraph) {
  console.log('\n--- Testing by lines within the hanging paragraph ---')
  const lines = hangParagraph.split('\n')
  console.log(`Lines in paragraph: ${lines.length}`)

  for (let i = 0; i < lines.length; i++) {
    const partial = lines.slice(0, i + 1).join('\n')
    const withContext = beforeHang + '\n\n' + partial
    result = await tryCompile(withContext, `with lines 0-${i}`)
    if (result.timedOut) {
      console.log(`🚨 Adding line ${i} causes hang: ${lines[i]?.slice(0, 200)}`)
      // Test without context
      result = await tryCompile(partial, `lines 0-${i} alone`)
      if (result.timedOut) {
        console.log(`   🚨 Also hangs alone`)
      } else {
        console.log(`   ✅ OK alone (${result.elapsed.toFixed(0)}ms) - context dependent`)
      }
      break
    } else {
      if (result.elapsed > 100) {
        console.log(`⚠️  Line ${i} slow: ${result.elapsed.toFixed(0)}ms`)
      }
    }
  }
}

import { readFileSync } from 'fs'
import { fileURLToPath } from 'url'
import { dirname, join } from 'path'
import { Worker, isMainThread, parentPort, workerData } from 'worker_threads'

const __dirname = dirname(fileURLToPath(import.meta.url))

if (!isMainThread) {
  const { mode, input, pattern, flags } = workerData
  if (mode === 'compile') {
    const { compiler } = await import('markdown-to-jsx')
    const start = performance.now()
    try { compiler(input) } catch {}
    parentPort.postMessage({ elapsed: performance.now() - start })
  } else if (mode === 'regex') {
    const regex = new RegExp(pattern, flags)
    const start = performance.now()
    regex.exec(input)
    parentPort.postMessage({ elapsed: performance.now() - start })
  }
  process.exit(0)
}

const testMd = readFileSync(join(__dirname, 'test.md'), 'utf-8')
const TIMEOUT_MS = 5000

async function runWorker(workerData, label) {
  return new Promise((resolve) => {
    const worker = new Worker(fileURLToPath(import.meta.url), { workerData })
    const timer = setTimeout(() => {
      worker.terminate()
      resolve({ timedOut: true, label })
    }, TIMEOUT_MS)
    worker.on('message', (msg) => {
      clearTimeout(timer)
      resolve({ ...msg, timedOut: false, label })
    })
    worker.on('error', (err) => {
      clearTimeout(timer)
      resolve({ error: err.message, timedOut: false, label })
    })
  })
}

import { readFileSync } from 'fs'
import { fileURLToPath } from 'url'
import { dirname, join } from 'path'
import { Worker, isMainThread, parentPort, workerData } from 'worker_threads'

const __dirname = dirname(fileURLToPath(import.meta.url))

if (!isMainThread) {
  const { mode, input, pattern, flags } = workerData
  if (mode === 'compile') {
    const { compiler } = await import('markdown-to-jsx')
    const start = performance.now()
    try { compiler(input) } catch {}
    parentPort.postMessage({ elapsed: performance.now() - start })
  } else if (mode === 'regex') {
    const regex = new RegExp(pattern, flags)
    const start = performance.now()
    regex.exec(input)
    parentPort.postMessage({ elapsed: performance.now() - start })
  }
  process.exit(0)
}

const testMd = readFileSync(join(__dirname, 'test.md'), 'utf-8')
const lines = testMd.split('\n')
const TIMEOUT_MS = 3000

async function run(workerData, label) {
  return new Promise((resolve) => {
    const worker = new Worker(fileURLToPath(import.meta.url), { workerData })
    const timer = setTimeout(() => {
      worker.terminate()
      resolve({ timedOut: true, label })
    }, TIMEOUT_MS)
    worker.on('message', (msg) => {
      clearTimeout(timer)
      resolve({ ...msg, timedOut: false, label })
    })
    worker.on('error', (err) => {
      clearTimeout(timer)
      resolve({ error: err.message, timedOut: false, label })
    })
  })
}

    hi = mid
  } else {

Very interesting! The `HTML_BLOCK_R` regex alone is fine on all subsets (38 tests all passed), and removing line 733 still hangs. So my previous binary search result was misleading - line 733 isn't THE critical line; it's just where the exponential behavior crosses the threshold.

The inner content alone compiles fine (13ms), but wrapping it in `<nested-doc>...</nested-doc>` makes it hang when compiled. This means:

```mjs
import { readFileSync } from 'fs'
import { fileURLToPath } from 'url'
import { dirname, join } from 'path'
import { Worker, isMainThread, parentPort, workerData } from 'worker_threads'

const __dirname = dirname(fileURLToPath(import.meta.url))

if (!isMainThread) {
  const { input } = workerData

  // Import and monkey-patch the compiler
  const mod = await import('markdown-to-jsx')
  const { compiler, RuleType } = mod

  // We need to intercept the internal rules. The compiler function creates rules internally.
  // Let's patch RegExp.prototype.exec with a per-call timeout detection using SharedArrayBuffer

  // Simpler approach: patch RegExp.prototype.exec to track which patterns are slow
  const origExec = RegExp.prototype.exec
  let currentCall = { pattern: '', start: 0 }
  let slowCalls = []

  // Set up a watchdog that checks every 100ms
  const watchdogInterval = setInterval(() => {
    if (currentCall.start > 0) {
      const elapsed = performance.now() - currentCall.start
      if (elapsed > 2000) {
        // This regex is hanging!
        parentPort.postMessage({
          type: 'hang',
          pattern: currentCall.pattern,
          elapsed,
          inputPreview: currentCall.inputPreview,
          inputLen: currentCall.inputLen
        })
        process.exit(1)
      }
    }
  }, 100)

  RegExp.prototype.exec = function(str) {
    const pattern = this.toString()
    const inputLen = typeof str === 'string' ? str.length : 0
    const inputPreview = typeof str === 'string' ? str.slice(0, 200) : ''

    currentCall = { pattern, start: performance.now(), inputPreview, inputLen }
    const result = origExec.call(this, str)
    const elapsed = performance.now() - currentCall.start
    currentCall = { pattern: '', start: 0 }

    if (elapsed > 50) {
      slowCalls.push({ pattern, elapsed, inputLen, inputPreview })
    }

    return result
  }

  try {
    compiler(input)
    clearInterval(watchdogInterval)
    RegExp.prototype.exec = origExec
    parentPort.postMessage({ type: 'done', slowCalls })
  } catch (e) {
    clearInterval(watchdogInterval)
    RegExp.prototype.exec = origExec
    parentPort.postMessage({ type: 'error', message: e.message, slowCalls })
  }

  process.exit(0)
}

const testMd = readFileSync(join(__dirname, 'test.md'), 'utf-8')
const allLines = testMd.split('\n')
const TIMEOUT_MS = 10000

// Build the second <nested-doc> block
const block2 = allLines.slice(825, 1592).join('\n')

console.log(`Testing block 2 (${block2.length} chars) with instrumented compiler...`)

const worker = new Worker(fileURLToPath(import.meta.url), {
  workerData: { input: block2 }
})

const timer = setTimeout(() => {
  worker.terminate()
  console.log('🚨 Worker terminated after timeout')
}, TIMEOUT_MS)

worker.on('message', (msg) => {
  if (msg.type === 'hang') {
    clearTimeout(timer)
    console.log(`\n🚨 REGEX HANG DETECTED!`)
    console.log(`Pattern: ${msg.pattern}`)
    console.log(`Elapsed: ${msg.elapsed.toFixed(0)}ms (and still running)`)
    console.log(`Input length: ${msg.inputLen}`)
    console.log(`Input preview: ${msg.inputPreview}`)
    worker.terminate()
  } else if (msg.type === 'done') {
    clearTimeout(timer)
    console.log('✅ Compilation completed')
    if (msg.slowCalls?.length) {
      console.log('\nSlow regex calls:')
      for (const call of msg.slowCalls.sort((a, b) => b.elapsed - a.elapsed)) {
        console.log(`  ${call.elapsed.toFixed(0)}ms | pattern: ${call.pattern.slice(0, 120)}`)
        console.log(`         input (${call.inputLen} chars): ${call.inputPreview.slice(0, 120)}...`)
      }
    }
  } else if (msg.type === 'error') {
    clearTimeout(timer)
    console.log(`Error: ${msg.message}`)
    if (msg.slowCalls?.length) {
      console.log('\nSlow calls before error:')
      for (const call of msg.slowCalls) {
        console.log(`  ${call.elapsed.toFixed(0)}ms | ${call.pattern.slice(0, 100)}`)
      }
    }
  }
})

worker.on('error', (err) => {
  clearTimeout(timer)
  console.error('Worker error:', err.message)
})

import { readFileSync } from 'fs'
import { fileURLToPath } from 'url'
import { dirname, join } from 'path'
import { Worker, isMainThread, parentPort, workerData } from 'worker_threads'

const __dirname = dirname(fileURLToPath(import.meta.url))

if (!isMainThread) {
  const { mode, input, pattern, flags } = workerData
  if (mode === 'regex-capture') {
    const regex = new RegExp(pattern, flags)
    const result = regex.exec(input)
    if (result) {
      parentPort.postMessage({
        matched: true,
        fullMatchLen: result[0].length,
        groups: result.slice(1).map((g, i) => ({
          index: i + 1,
          len: g?.length || 0,
          preview: g?.slice(0, 100) + (g?.length > 100 ? '...' : ''),
          tail: g?.slice(-100)
        }))
      })
    } else {
      parentPort.postMessage({ matched: false })
    }
  } else if (mode === 'compile') {
    const { compiler } = await import('markdown-to-jsx')
    const start = performance.now()
    try { compiler(input) } catch (e) { parentPort.postMessage({ error: e.message }) }
    parentPort.postMessage({ elapsed: performance.now() - start })
  }
  process.exit(0)
}

const testMd = readFileSync(join(__dirname, 'test.md'), 'utf-8')
const allLines = testMd.split('\n')
const TIMEOUT_MS = 3000

async function run(wd) {
  return new Promise((resolve) => {
    const worker = new Worker(fileURLToPath(import.meta.url), { workerData: wd })
    const timer = setTimeout(() => { worker.terminate(); resolve({ timedOut: true }) }, TIMEOUT_MS)
    worker.on('message', (msg) => { clearTimeout(timer); resolve(msg) })
    worker.on('error', (e) => { clearTimeout(timer); resolve({ error: e.message }) })
  })
}

const block2 = allLines.slice(825, 1592).join('\n')
const htmlBlockP = '^ *(?!<[a-z][^ >/]* ?\\/>)<([a-z][^ >/]*) ?((?:[^>]*[^/])?)>\\n?(\\s*(?:<\\1[^>]*?>[\\s\\S]*?<\\/\\1>|(?!<\\1\\b)[\\s\\S])*?)<\\/\\1>(?!<\\/\\1>)\\n*'

console.log('=== HTML_BLOCK_R capture analysis on block 2 ===\n')
let result = await run({ mode: 'regex-capture', input: block2, pattern: htmlBlockP, flags: 'i' })
if (result.timedOut) {
  console.log('🚨 Regex timed out')
} else if (result.matched) {
  console.log(`Full match length: ${result.fullMatchLen} (block length: ${block2.length})`)
  for (const g of result.groups) {
    console.log(`  Group ${g.index}: ${g.len} chars`)
    console.log(`    Start: ${g.preview}`)
    if (g.tail) console.log(`    End: ...${g.tail}`)
  }
  const remainingLen = block2.length - result.fullMatchLen
  console.log(`\nRemaining after match: ${remainingLen} chars`)
  if (remainingLen > 0) {
    const remaining = block2.slice(result.fullMatchLen)
    console.log(`Remaining preview: ${remaining.slice(0, 200)}`)

    // Test compiling just the remaining
    console.log('\nCompiling remaining...')
    const r = await run({ mode: 'compile', input: remaining })
    if (r.timedOut) {
      console.log('🚨 Remaining content HANGS')
    } else {
      console.log(`✅ Remaining OK (${r.elapsed?.toFixed(0)}ms)`)
    }
  }
} else {
  console.log('No match! HTML_BLOCK_R does not match block 2')
  console.log('Block 2 starts with:', block2.slice(0, 100))

  // Maybe the parser sees it differently. Let's check what happens
  // when the parser encounters <nested-doc> - maybe it's matched by HTML_SELF_CLOSING
  const selfClosingP = '^ *<([a-z][a-z0-9:]*)(?:\\s+((?:<.*?>|[^>])*))?\\/>(?!<\\/\\1>)(\\s*\\n)?'
  result = await run({ mode: 'regex-capture', input: block2, pattern: selfClosingP, flags: 'i' })
  console.log('HTML_SELF_CLOSING match:', result.matched)

  // Test with HTML_COMMENT
  const commentP = '^<!--[\\s\\S]*?(?:-->)'
  result = await run({ mode: 'regex-capture', input: block2, pattern: commentP, flags: '' })
  console.log('HTML_COMMENT match:', result.matched)
}

// Let me also check: what if the block is NOT matched as HTML at all?
// Then the parser would try to parse it as regular markdown, line by line.
// The <nested-doc> would be treated as inline HTML (HTML_SELF_CLOSING_R)
// and then the inner content would be parsed as markdown.

// Let me check: does the block 2 match ANY block-level rule?
console.log('\n=== Testing all block-level regexes on block 2 ===')
const blockRegexes = {
  'BLOCKQUOTE_R': '^(\\s*>[\\s\\S]*?)(?=\\n\\n|$)',
  'CODE_BLOCK_FENCED_R': '^(?: {1,3})?(`{3,}|~{3,}) *(\\S+)? *([^\\n]*?)?\\n([\\s\\S]*?)(?:\\1\\n?|$)',
  'CODE_BLOCK_R': '^(?: {4}[^\\n]+\\n*)+(?:\\n *)+\\n?',
  'HEADING_R': '^ *(#{1,6}) *([^\\n]+?)(?: +#*)?(?:\\n *)*(?:\\n|$)',
  'HEADING_SETEXT_R': '^([^\\n]+)\\n *(=|-){3,} *(?:\\n *)+\\n',
  'HTML_BLOCK_R': htmlBlockP,
  'HTML_SELF_CLOSING_R': '^ *<([a-z][a-z0-9:]*)(?:\\s+((?:<.*?>|[^>])*))?\\/>(?!<\\/\\1>)(\\s*\\n)?',
  'HTML_COMMENT_R': '^<!--[\\s\\S]*?(?:-->)',
  'NP_TABLE_R': '^(\\|.*)\\n(?: *(\\|? *[-:]+ *\\|[-| :]*)\\n((?:.*\\|.*\\n)*))?\\n?',
  'PARAGRAPH_R': '^[^\\n]+(?:  \\n|\\n{2,})',
  'BREAK_THEMATIC_R': '^(?:( *[-*_])){3,} *(?:\\n *)+\\n',
  'FOOTNOTE_R': '^\\[\\^([^\\]]+)](:(.*)((\n+ {4,}.*)|(\n(?!\\[\\^).+))*)',
  'FRONT_MATTER_R': '^---[ \\t]*\\n(.|\\n)*\\n---[ \\t]*\\n',
  'CONSECUTIVE_NEWLINE_R': '^(?:\\n *)*\\n',
  'REF_IMAGE_OR_LINK': '^\\[([^\\]]*)\\]:\\s+<?([^\\s>]+)>?\\s*("([^"]*)")?',
}

for (const [name, pattern] of Object.entries(blockRegexes)) {
  result = await run({ mode: 'regex-capture', input: block2, pattern, flags: name === 'HTML_BLOCK_R' || name === 'HTML_SELF_CLOSING_R' ? 'i' : '' })
  if (result.timedOut) {
    console.log(`🚨 ${name} TIMED OUT`)
  } else if (result.matched) {
    console.log(`✅ ${name} MATCHED (${result.fullMatchLen} chars)`)
  }
}

// Critical test: what does the parser see?
// If <nested-doc> is recognized as self-closing HTML, then the inner content

console.log('\n=== Testing: does <nested-doc> alone match self-closing? ===')
result = await run({ mode: 'regex-capture', input: '<nested-doc>\n', pattern: '^ *<([a-z][a-z0-9:]*)(?:\\s+((?:<.*?>|[^>])*))?\\/>(?!<\\/\\1>)(\\s*\\n)?', flags: 'i' })
console.log('Self-closing on "<nested-doc>\\n":', result.matched)

// Does it match paragraph?
result = await run({ mode: 'regex-capture', input: '<nested-doc>\n\n', pattern: '^[^\\n]+(?:  \\n|\\n{2,})', flags: '' })

3. It trims the whitespace

The key difference is: `containsBlockSyntax` - if this returns false, the content is parsed as **inline**, which means the inline rules (TEXT_BOLD_R, TEXT_EMPHASIZED_R, etc.) will be applied to the ENTIRE inner content at once. This means block-level rules (heading, paragraph, table, etc.) are used AND inline rules are also used (since `state.inline` being false doesn't disable inline rules - it enables them along with block rules).

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-03-11 12:54:30Z)**_

## 结论

V8 profiler 明确指出，**98.9%** 之 CPU 时间卡于 `TEXT_EMPHASIZED_R` 正则：

```
^([*_])((?:\[.*?\][([].*?[)\]]|<.*?>(?:.*?<.*?>)?|`.*?`|\\\1|[\s\S])+?)\1(?!\1)
```

此即 `@innei/markdown-to-jsx-yet` 中用于匹配 `*斜体*` / `_斜体_` 之正则。其核心为 `INLINE_SKIP_R`：

```
((?:\[.*?\][([].*?[)\]]|<.*?>(?:.*?<.*?>)?|`.*?`|\\\1|[\s\S])+?)
```

**致命原因**：`[\s\S]`（匹配任意字符）与前面之 alternative（`\[.*?\]`、`<.*?>`、`` `.*?` ``）存在**重叠**。外层 `()+?` lazy quantifier 意味着引擎遇到不匹配之闭合 delimiter 时，须回溯尝试所有可能的字符分配方式，复杂度呈指数级增长。

**触发条件**：第二个 `<nested-doc>` block（line 825-1591，~12000 字符）被 `HTML_BLOCK_ELEMENT_R` 匹配后，内部内容经 `parseBlock` 逐段解析。某段内容以 `*` 或 `_` 开头但后续无匹配闭合 delimiter，使该正则在长 input 上陷入 catastrophic backtracking。

**修复方向**：
1. 在 `@innei/markdown-to-jsx-yet` fork 中，将 `INLINE_SKIP_R` 替换为非回溯模式，如 `([\\s\\S]+?)`（君之自定义 parser 已如此处理）
2. 或使用 `disableParsingRawHTML` 避免 `<nested-doc>` 被解析为 HTML block
3. 或在 feed 渲染前预处理，将 `<nested-doc>` 替换为 code block 等安全结构

---

```mjs
import { readFileSync, writeFileSync } from 'fs'

const dir = '<REPO_ROOT>/node_modules/.pnpm/@innei+markdown-to-jsx-yet@7.7.7-11_react@19.2.4/node_modules/@innei/markdown-to-jsx-yet/dist'

// The exact string from the built file (verified via node inspection):
// J="((?:\\[.*?\\][([].*?[)\\]]|<.*?>(?:.*?<.*?>)?|`.*?`|\\\\\\1|[\\s\\S])+?)"
const OLD = `((?:\\\\[.*?\\\\][([].*?[)\\\\]]|<.*?>(?:.*?<.*?>)?|\`.*?\`|\\\\\\\\\\\\1|[\\\\s\\\\S])+?)`
const NEW = `([\\\\s\\\\S]+?)`

for (const file of ['index.module.js', 'index.modern.js', 'index.cjs', 'index.umd.js']) {
  const p = `${dir}/${file}`
  let src
  try {
    src = readFileSync(p, 'utf-8')
  } catch {
    console.log(`${file}: not found, skipping`)
    continue
  }

  const idx = src.indexOf(OLD)
  if (idx >= 0) {
    const patched = src.replace(OLD, NEW)
    // Verify replacement
    if (patched.indexOf(OLD) === -1 && patched.indexOf(NEW) >= 0) {
      writeFileSync(p, patched)
      console.log(`${file}: patched successfully (at index ${idx})`)
    } else {
      console.log(`${file}: replacement verification failed`)
    }
  } else {
    console.log(`${file}: OLD pattern not found`)
    // Debug: show what's around the expected location
    const altIdx = src.indexOf('(?:\\\\[.*?\\\\]')
    if (altIdx >= 0) {
      console.log(`  Found fragment at ${altIdx}: ${src.substring(altIdx - 10, altIdx + 80)}`)
    }
  }
}

```

```mjs
import { readFileSync } from 'fs'
import { fileURLToPath } from 'url'
import { dirname, join } from 'path'
import { Worker, isMainThread, parentPort, workerData } from 'worker_threads'

const __dirname = dirname(fileURLToPath(import.meta.url))

if (!isMainThread) {
  const { compiler } = await import('markdown-to-jsx')
  const start = performance.now()
  try { compiler(workerData.input) } catch (e) { parentPort.postMessage({ error: e.message }) }
  parentPort.postMessage({ elapsed: performance.now() - start })
  process.exit(0)
}

const testMd = readFileSync(join(__dirname, 'test.md'), 'utf-8')

async function run(input, label) {
  return new Promise((resolve) => {
    const worker = new Worker(fileURLToPath(import.meta.url), { workerData: { input } })
    const timer = setTimeout(() => { worker.terminate(); resolve({ timedOut: true }) }, 10000)
    worker.on('message', (msg) => {
      if (msg.error) console.log(`  (error: ${msg.error})`)
      if (msg.elapsed != null) { clearTimeout(timer); resolve(msg) }
    })
    worker.on('error', (e) => { clearTimeout(timer); resolve({ error: e.message }) })
  })
}

console.log('Testing full test.md after patch...')
const result = await run(testMd, 'full')
if (result.timedOut) {
  console.log('🚨 Still HANGS!')
} else if (result.error) {
  console.log(`Error: ${result.error}`)
} else {
  console.log(`✅ Compiled in ${result.elapsed.toFixed(0)}ms`)
}

```

```

import { readFileSync, writeFileSync } from 'fs'

const dir = '<REPO_ROOT>/node_modules/.pnpm_patches/@innei/markdown-to-jsx-yet@7.7.7-11/dist'

const OLD = `((?:\\\\[.*?\\\\][([].*?[)\\\\]]|<.*?>(?:.*?<.*?>)?|\`.*?\`|\\\\\\\\\\\\1|[\\\\s\\\\S])+?)`
const NEW = `([\\\\s\\\\S]+?)`

for (const file of ['index.module.js', 'index.modern.js', 'index.cjs', 'index.umd.js']) {
  const p = `${dir}/${file}`
  let src
  try { src = readFileSync(p, 'utf-8') } catch { console.log(`${file}: skip`); continue }
  const idx = src.indexOf(OLD)
  if (idx >= 0) {
    writeFileSync(p, src.replace(OLD, NEW))
    console.log(`${file}: patched`)
  } else {
    console.log(`${file}: not found`)
  }
}

// Also patch the source index.tsx

const NEW_SRC = `'([\\\\s\\\\S]+?)'`
if (tsx.includes(OLD_SRC)) {
  writeFileSync(tsxPath, tsx.replace(OLD_SRC, NEW_SRC))
  console.log('index.tsx: patched')
} else {
  // Try finding the line directly
  const line = "  '((?:\\\\[.*?\\\\][([].*?[)\\\\]]|<.*?>(?:.*?<.*?>)?|`.*?`|\\\\\\\\\\1|[\\s\\S])+?)'"
  const idx2 = tsx.indexOf(line)
  console.log('index.tsx: alt search at', idx2)
}
